title: Forest fire contained in Udhampur’s Dabbar area amid heatwave warning - The News Mill
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Jammu and Kashmir
full_text:
A forest fire broke out in a compartment of the Dabbar area near Flata village in Udhampur district, prompting an immediate response from the Forest Department on June 6.
Range Officer Ayush Gupta said teams were deployed to the site promptly after receiving information. Gupta had been managing another fire in the Tirchi forest area of Udhampur, which has now been brought under control, before moving to oversee containment efforts at Dabbar. He added that efforts were ongoing to control the fire.
The Forest Department issued a public appeal in light of a three-to-four-day heatwave advisory from the Meteorological Department. Residents were urged not to light fires near forested areas, as such blazes cause substantial damage to wildlife, birds, and the forest ecosystem.
Officials indicated that the fire is almost extinguished and expressed optimism that if current stable weather conditions persist without strong winds, the fire will soon be fully controlled.
Earlier on Friday, a large fire erupted in the Bindi forest area of Kalalkass Block under Kandi Range in Rajouri district, prompting a significant response from the Forest Department and allied agencies, an official said.
Officials attributed the rise in forest fire incidents across Rajouri district to the ongoing heatwave and dry weather conditions. Firefighting teams continue working on site to contain the flames and limit damage to forest resources.
Rajesh Khajuria, a forest official, expressed gratitude to local villagers for their assistance in extinguishing the fire. He recalled: “The fire broke out at noon; I found out via a phone call and rushed here immediately. The villagers stood by me and offered immense support. Everyone came together to extinguish the forest fire. With the help of these villagers, we managed to save the forest. The people of Bhindi, the people of my area, are truly wonderful and always cooperative; they extend their full support to anyone from the Forest Department. It is thanks to their cooperation that we were able to put out the fire and save the forest.”

title: Massive fire erupts in Rajouri's Bindi forest area amid heatwave - Dailyhunt
search_term: heatwave (Anantnag OR Bandipore OR Baramulla OR Budgam OR Doda OR Ganderbal OR Jammu OR Kathua OR Kishtwar OR Kulgam OR Kupwara OR Poonch OR Pulwama OR Rajouri OR Ramban OR Reasi OR Samba OR Shopian OR Srinagar OR Udhampur) Jammu and Kashmir
full_text:
A large fire broke out in the Bindi forest area of Kalalkass Block under Kandi Range in Rajouri district on June 5, prompting a widespread response from the Forest Department and allied agencies, an official stated.
Teams from the Forest Department, Forest Protection Force (FPF), and local residents actively worked to contain the blaze and prevent it from spreading to neighbouring forest areas.
Officials attributed the increase in forest fire incidents across Rajouri district to ongoing heatwave and dry weather conditions. Firefighting personnel continue to operate on the ground to control the flames and reduce damage to forest resources.
Rajesh Khajuria, a forest official, thanked villagers for their assistance in extinguishing the fire, which broke out at noon. He said, “The fire broke out at noon; I found out via a phone call and rushed here immediately. The villagers stood by me and offered immense support. Everyone came together to extinguish the forest fire. With the help of these villagers, we managed to save the forest. The people of Bhindi, the people of my area, are truly wonderful and always cooperative; they extend their full support to anyone from the Forest Department. It is thanks to their cooperation that we were able to put out the fire and save the forest.”
Authorities have urged the public to stay alert and report any forest fire incidents immediately to the relevant departments to ensure a prompt response.
